info.name: MDM MVP - Customer Management
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for testing the MDM (Master Data Management) MVP system with CQRS architecture.

## Architecture
- **Customer Ingestion Service** (port 8080) — Command side (writes via Kafka)
- **Customer Mastering Service** (port 8081) — Query side (reads from PostgreSQL)
- **OAuth2 Server** (port 9999) — JWT token issuance (RS256)
- **Kafka** (port 9092) — Event streaming (KRaft mode)
- **PostgreSQL** (port 5432) — Golden record storage

## Key Concepts
- **nationalId** is the canonical unique identifier for customer deduplication
- Deduplication is by **nationalId exact match** (not email)
- Kafka partition key = nationalId (guarantees per-customer ordering)
- Dual-key idempotency: client-provided + SHA-256(nationalId|sourceSystem)
- DLQ (Dead Letter Queue) for poison messages after retry exhaustion

## Collection Structure
1. **OAuth2 Authentication** — Get JWT tokens
2. **Health & Setup** — Service health checks
3. **Command Side (Write)** — Customer ingestion with nationalId
4. **Query Side (Read)** — Customer queries by nationalId
5. **Idempotency Testing** — Duplicate detection
6. **Validation & Errors** — Error handling
7. **DLQ & Retry** — Dead Letter Queue testing
8. **Observability** — Metrics & SLOs

## Environment Variables
- `base_url_ingestion`: http://localhost:8080
- `base_url_mastering`: http://localhost:8081
- `oauth_url`: http://localhost:9999
- `oauth_token`: JWT token (auto-set)
- `test_national_id`: Test national ID (auto-set)
- `test_customer_id`: Golden record ID (auto-set)

item.name: 03-Conflict-Resolution-Scenarios
Scenario-1-LATEST-Update

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description: Send customer event from CRM with older timestamp. Email: john@old.com @ 10:30:00

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description: Send conflicting event from BANK with newer timestamp. Email: john@new.com @ 10:30:05. Expected: BANK email wins (LATEST_UPDATE strategy)
Verify that email=john@new.com (BANK's value won due to later timestamp)

item.description: Test LATEST_UPDATE strategy: Most recent timestamp wins. CRM sends email@old.com @ 10:30:00, BANK sends email@new.com @ 10:30:05 → Result: email@new.com

item.name: Scenario-2-TRUSTED-Source
Step 1 - Send CRM Event (Trusted for Name)

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description: Send customer event from CRM (trusted source for name field)

item.name: Step 2 - Send BANK Event (Newer but Not Trusted for Name)

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description: Send conflicting event from BANK. Name: J. Doe (newer but BANK not trusted for name). Expected: Name stays 'John Michael Doe' (CRM is trusted)
Verify that name='John Michael Doe' (CRM trusted), but email='john@bank.com' (LATEST_UPDATE for email)

item.description: Test TRUSTED_SOURCE strategy: CRM is trusted for name field. CRM says 'John Michael Doe', BANK says 'J. Doe' → Result: 'John Michael Doe' (CRM trusted)

Verify phone field contains JSON array: ["+1-555-1111","+1-555-2222","+1-555-3333"]

item.description: Test MERGE strategy for phones: Multiple sources provide different phone numbers → Result: All phones merged into array with UNION strategy (max 5)
Conflict resolution test scenarios to verify different strategies

exec: console.log("Duplicate submitted with same nationalId - should be detected by mastering service");

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description:
Submit duplicate with SAME nationalId but different name/email/phone.
The mastering service will detect this as a duplicate via nationalId exact match and UPDATE the existing golden record (coalesce merge strategy).

exec: console.log("Idempotent replay detected - same nationalId + sourceSystem returns cached eventId");

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope
Optional client-provided idempotency key

request.description:
Submit the EXACT same nationalId + sourceSystem combination.

The ingestion service will detect this via the deterministic idempotency key SHA-256(nationalId|sourceSystem) and return 200 OK with status=CACHED (not 202 ACCEPTED).

First submission → 202 ACCEPTED
Replay → 200 OK (CACHED, with X-Idempotency-Replay: true header)

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope
Optional client-provided idempotency key

originalRequest.description:
Submit the EXACT same nationalId + sourceSystem combination.

The ingestion service will detect this via the deterministic idempotency key SHA-256(nationalId|sourceSystem) and return 200 OK with status=CACHED (not 202 ACCEPTED).

First submission → 202 ACCEPTED
Replay → 200 OK (CACHED, with X-Idempotency-Replay: true header)

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope
Client-provided idempotency key

request.description:
Submit with a client-provided X-Idempotency-Key header.

Replaying the same key will return the cached result (200 OK, status=CACHED) instead of creating a duplicate event.

TTL: 24 hours for client-provided keys.

Query the golden record after duplicate submission.
The version should be incremented and fields merged via coalesce strategy.
nationalId is required. Should return 400 Bad Request.

originalRequest.description: nationalId is required. Should return 400 Bad Request.

request.description: nationalId must be 12-13 alphanumeric characters. Should return 400.

request.description: sourceSystem must be one of: BANK, GOVERNMENT, CRM, MOBILE, WEB, PARTNER. Should return 400.
Missing Content-Type header should return 415 Unsupported Media Type.
Missing OAuth2 token should return 401 Unauthorized.

item.name: Get Non-Existent Customer By National ID (404)

request.description: Get customer by non-existent nationalId should return 404.

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_READ scope

request.description:
Get DLQ topic configuration and retry settings.

Requires OAuth2 token with CUSTOMER_READ or ADMIN role.

Returns:
- DLQ and raw topic names
- Retry configuration (max attempts, intervals, multiplier)
- Error type classifications

This replaces the docker-compose exec kafka kafka-topics --list command.

response.name: DLQ Stats Response

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_READ scope

request.description:
Get DLQ message structure reference with examples.

Requires OAuth2 token with CUSTOMER_READ or ADMIN role.

Returns:
- Complete DLQ message JSON example
- Field descriptions
- Error type classifications with examples and behavior

This replaces the need to manually inspect DLQ messages to understand the format.

item.name: DLQ Reprocess - Send DLQ Message for Reprocessing

exec: console.log("Reprocessed with new event ID: " + jsonData.newEventId);

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE or ADMIN role

request.description:
Reprocess a DLQ message by extracting the original event and republishing it to the customer.raw topic.

Requires OAuth2 token with CUSTOMER_WRITE or ADMIN role.

The service will:
1. Extract the originalEvent from the DLQ wrapper
2. Generate a new eventId for tracking
3. Validate required fields (nationalId, sourceSystem)
4. Publish to customer.raw topic for reprocessing
5. Return the new eventId for tracking

Response includes:
- success: boolean
- newEventId: UUID for the reprocessed event
- nationalId: customer identifier
- sourceSystem: original source
- republishedTo: Kafka topic name

response.name: Reprocess Error Response

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_READ scope

request.description:
Get step-by-step instructions for manually reprocessing DLQ messages using Kafka CLI tools.

Requires OAuth2 token with CUSTOMER_READ or ADMIN role.

Instructions include:
1. Consuming DLQ messages
2. Extracting original event
3. Publishing back to raw topic
4. Monitoring reprocessing

Also includes alternative HTTP API approach.

NOTE: This request retrieves DLQ topic information via the HTTP API.

To list Kafka topics from the command line, run:
  ./tests/dlq-manage.sh list-topics

Expected topics:
- customer.raw (incoming events)
- customer.mastered (processed golden records)
- customer.dlq (failed events)

NOTE: To consume DLQ messages from the command line, run:
  ./tests/dlq-manage.sh consume-dlq 10

This will display up to 10 DLQ messages from the customer.dlq topic.

DLQ messages contain the original event, error details, processing history, and schema version.

item.name: Retry - Check Mastering Service Logs (Shell Script)

request.description:
NOTE: To view retry-related logs from the command line, run:
  ./tests/dlq-manage.sh logs-retry 100

Or view all logs:
  ./tests/dlq-manage.sh logs-all 100

Look for:
- 'Retry attempt for event' — retry in progress
- 'Sent to DLQ' — all retries exhausted
- 'Successfully processed event' — normal processing

item.name: Retry - View DLQ Statistics from Metrics

request.description:
Get DLQ statistics from Prometheus metrics.

This shows the total number of failed events that were sent to the DLQ.

To get comprehensive DLQ stats including configuration, use:
  GET {{base_url_mastering}}/api/dlq/stats

Or from command line:
  ./tests/dlq-manage.sh stats

Get detailed health status including processing metrics:
- totalProcessed: total events processed
- totalDuplicates: duplicates detected
- duplicateRate: percentage of duplicates

SLO Target: 99.9% availability

originalRequest.description:
Get detailed health status including processing metrics:
- totalProcessed: total events processed
- totalDuplicates: duplicates detected
- duplicateRate: percentage of duplicates

SLO Target: 99.9% availability

request.description: Get error count for burn rate calculation.

Get latency histogram for p50/p95/p99 calculation.

SLO: 99% of events processed in < 1 second.

Get deduplication lookup latency.

SLO: 99% of lookups < 10ms (nationalId indexed).
